summary: Typosquat of @solana-labs/anchor exfiltrates host info and executes shell commands at install
details: The package masquerades as the legitimate @solana-labs/anchor toolkit (note the 'ancor' misspelling) and ships an install.js executed via the package.json postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js"). On install, install.js gathers host identifiers using os.hostname() and os.userInfo(), reads filesystem state via fs.existsSync at multiple paths, and POSTs the collected data over HTTPS. It also invokes execSync to run powershell and curl commands at install time, providing arbitrary command execution on the installer's machine. The combination of automatic execution via npm postinstall, system-information collection, network exfiltration, and shell-out via child_process is a classic install-time supply-chain attack against developers who mistype the legitimate Solana Anchor package name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
